frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis (MVPA)
frameworks.one_line: decode TMS parameters from spatial BOLD patterns at the stimulation site

frameworks.name: univariate BOLD activation
frameworks.one_line: mean-activity readout fails to register the TMS-induced effect

frameworks.name: causal perturbation via TMS
frameworks.one_line: TMS used to test causal contributions of specific cortical regions

core_question: How do TMS intensity and frequency modulate concurrently measured BOLD signals at the stimulation site?
key_assumptions: concurrent TMS-fMRI yields interpretable BOLD at the stimulation site
multivoxel patterns can index neural changes invisible to mean activity
motor threshold provides a meaningful intensity reference for DLPFC
manipulations: TMS intensity: high (100% MT) vs low (50% MT)
key_findings: TMS intensity decodable from multivoxel BOLD patterns at the site
TMS frequency decodable from multivoxel BOLD patterns at the site
no reliable change in overall mean BOLD activity at the stimulation site in either experiment
interpretations: TMS influences neural activity in a pattern-level rather than mean-activity manner
multivariate readouts give insight into TMS mechanism that univariate analyses miss
